source: League Start Wealth Plan: A Guide for Profit Crafting & Investing (3.29 Update)
rules: Recalculate the full input cost, realistic sale price, and sale depth before starting a batch.
Use live completed-item listings and several comparable items; never price from the cheapest listing alone.
Set a maximum loss per attempt and keep the operating bankroll outside the craft.
List partial successes and useful misses instead of treating every non-perfect outcome as zero.
Do one proof craft before buying a large batch of bases or materials.
All searches, purchases, crafts, listings, whispers, and trades are manual.

guides.name: Focused +4 spell amulet
guides.capital: Very high; the +2 all-spell roll is approximately 1/900 and the elemental augment can brick.
guides.roi_thesis: Focused Amulet magnifies a small number of affixes, so +2 all spells plus a matching elemental gem-level prefix creates a scarce +4-style end product with premium caster suffixes.
requirements: Enough liquid currency to survive the alteration/chaos or essence variance.
Access to cannot roll attack modifiers, Harvest augment, prefixes cannot be changed, and the chosen suffix finish.

steps.action: Use Chaos Orbs or a cheap Screaming Essence that supplies a useful suffix until +2 to Level of all Spell Skill Gems appears.
steps.checkpoint: The guide estimates roughly 1/900 attempts; re-price the method before starting.

steps.action: Annul until +2 all Spell Skill Gems is the only remaining affix.
steps.checkpoint: If the +2 mod is removed, stop and restart from the base-roll step.

steps.action: Craft Cannot roll Attack Modifiers, then Harvest augment the desired element.
steps.checkpoint: The guide describes this as a 50/50 brick point. Do not proceed unless the expected finished value covers failed attempts.

steps.action: Craft Prefixes Cannot Be Changed and finish suffixes with reforge Critical for critical multiplier or reforge Caster for cast speed.
steps.checkpoint: The guide estimates 1/12 for T1 critical multiplier or about 1/2 for T2+ cast speed.

steps.action: Finish only after checking the exact buyer archetype; an optional reflecting-mist finish belongs in a separate, capped-risk batch.
steps.checkpoint: Price the deterministic amulet and the mist outcome separately.

selloffs: A clean +2 all-spell Focused Amulet can be sold rather than risked.
A successful matching-element prefix with a non-perfect suffix may still be a premium sale.
Do not continue metacrafting a suffix combination with no current ladder buyer.
guides.stop_loss: Cap attempts before rolling. Stop the batch if the observed base-roll cost or clean +2 resale price no longer supports the 50/50 augment risk.

guides.name: Helical attribute/crit ring
guides.market: Attribute stackers and premium crit jewellery
guides.capital: High; bases and repeated Deafening Essences are expensive.
guides.roi_thesis: Helical Ring amplifies a compact affix set. Critical multiplier plus T1 attributes, chaos resistance, and crafted all attributes sells into multiple high-budget stacker archetypes.
requirements: Deafening Essence of Scorn or the currently valuable alternative essence.
Access to suffix protection and the 3.29 attribute-reroll/ghost craft if using that branch.

steps.action: Essence of Scorn for critical strike multiplier until a T1 Strength, Dexterity, or Intelligence roll appears.
steps.checkpoint: Double-attribute outcomes are premium; price every good attribute combination before rerolling.

steps.action: If the 3.29 ghost craft can economically change the attribute to the demanded one, compare that cost with continuing the essence roll.
steps.checkpoint: Verify the live 3.29 ghost-crafting behavior and price before using it.

steps.action: If the remaining suffix is already useful, finish with the all-attributes craft.
steps.checkpoint: Leave the required suffix open; do not fill it accidentally.

steps.action: For a weak remaining suffix, clean the prefixes as required, craft Suffixes Cannot Be Changed, and use add/remove Chaos toward chaos resistance.
steps.checkpoint: Repeat only while the protected suffix value exceeds the metacraft and Harvest spend.

selloffs: Critical multiplier plus one T1 attribute is a sellable intermediate.
Unexpected double-attribute outcomes should be price-checked before further crafting.
A lower chaos-resistance tier can be the correct stop if another attempt has poor expected value.
guides.stop_loss: Stop essence rolling at the pre-declared spend or when a sellable attribute combination appears. Never expose the premium suffixes without protection.

guides.name: Fractured 35%-effect 12-passive large cluster
guides.market: High-budget aura, minion, bow, shield, and spell cluster jewels
guides.capital: High; item-level 84 bases and fracture attempts are expensive.
guides.roi_thesis: A fractured 35% increased effect modifier is the universal premium starting point for 12-passive clusters. The finishing method can rotate with the highest-value small-passive family.
requirements: Item-level 84+ Large Cluster Jewel with exactly 12 passives.
The correct small-passive family for the target market.
Either a fractured 35% effect base or a separately costed plan to create one.

steps.action: Choose the target family from current ladder demand: minion, bow, attack damage while holding a shield, or spell damage.
steps.checkpoint: Do not treat all 12-passive bases as interchangeable; the enchant family determines the buyer.

steps.action: Acquire 35% increased effect already fractured, or alteration-roll 35% effect and fracture only when the live fracture math is favorable.
steps.checkpoint: 3.29 removed beast splitting and changed fracture access. Verify the current fracture method before risking the base.

steps.action: For bow or shield families, reforge Speed toward 3% attack speed. For minion, use the guide's caster/speed finish toward 3% minion attack and cast speed. For spell damage, use a defence finish toward Energy Shield with Intelligence as the premium third stat.
steps.checkpoint: Price two-stat and three-stat results separately before rerolling.

steps.action: Divine only when the roll ranges materially change the sale bracket.
steps.checkpoint: Do not spend divines polishing a cluster whose secondary stats miss the meta.

selloffs: Fractured 35% effect by itself is a liquid premium base.
35% effect plus one T1 speed/defence stat is often sellable.
Unexpected high Energy Shield, Intelligence, attributes, or life rolls may fit a different buyer.
guides.stop_loss: Keep fracture creation and finishing as separate P&L decisions. Sell the clean fractured base if the finish no longer offers sufficient margin.

guides.name: Item-level 86 +2-arrow Spine Bow base
guides.market: Premium bow fracture and mirror-tier crafting inputs
guides.capital: Medium to high depending on base quality and fracture choice.
guides.roi_thesis: The shared guide estimates +2 arrows at roughly 1/591 alterations on an item-level 86 Spine Bow. Clean magic hits and fractured hits are valuable inputs for expensive bow projects.
requirements: Item-level 86+ Spine Bow; overquality has a separate premium.
Alteration/Augmentation budget and a plan to sell the clean magic hit.
A separately calculated fracture budget if attempting to upgrade the base.

steps.action: Roll a clean item-level 86 Spine Bow with alterations until Bow Attacks fire 2 additional Arrows.
steps.checkpoint: The guide estimates approximately 1/591 alterations. Track the actual batch cost.

steps.action: List the clean magic +2-arrow base, or compare its value with the expected cost and outcome value of the current fracture method.
steps.checkpoint: Selling the magic base is the low-variance branch; fracture creation is a new investment decision.

steps.action: If buying instead of rolling, use the live filter for already-fractured +2-arrow Spine Bows and verify quality, item level, and remaining mod state.
steps.checkpoint: Do not pay for irrelevant extra affixes that make the continuation harder.

selloffs: Already-fractured +2-arrow base when the spread to completed bows narrows.
guides.stop_loss: Stop at the alteration budget. Treat a fracture attempt as a separate capped bet rather than 'finishing' the alteration batch.

guides.name: T1 suppression Necrotic Armour base
guides.capital: Medium for alteration production; high for a fractured purchase.
guides.roi_thesis: The shared guide identifies item-level 86 Necrotic Armour with 22% spell suppression as a high-value fracture input, with the T1 roll estimated near 1/150 alterations.
requirements: Alteration budget or enough capital to buy the fractured T1 base.
A current buyer archetype that values both the base defences and suppression.

steps.action: Alteration-roll an item-level 86 Necrotic Armour until 22% chance to Suppress Spell Damage appears.
steps.checkpoint: The guide estimates approximately 1/150 alterations; verify that the magic hit still sells.

steps.action: Sell the clean T1-suppression magic base or separately price the current fracture route.
steps.checkpoint: Do not consume a profitable clean base in an underpriced fracture attempt.

steps.action: Use the fractured-base live search to buy mispriced inputs for a later Energy Shield or hybrid defence craft.
steps.checkpoint: Confirm the suppression value is 22% and fractured, not merely an ordinary explicit modifier.

selloffs: High-quality or well-rolled defence bases can command a separate premium.
guides.stop_loss: Stop at the alteration budget or sell the clean hit. Only fracture when the expected fractured-base value exceeds the full failure-adjusted cost.
